43	\section{Setup and motivation}
44	\label{sec:codim1_setup}
45	
46	Fix an algebraically closed field $\bar k$ throughout. By a \emph{variety} over $\bar k$
47	we mean a geometrically integral, separated scheme of finite type over $\bar k$
48	(Milne's convention, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, p.~v); a variety is \emph{nonsingular}
49	(or \emph{smooth}) if its local ring at every closed point is regular. A variety is
50	\emph{complete} if it is proper over $\bar k$.
51	
52	A \emph{rational map} $f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y$ between varieties is the equivalence
53	class of a regular morphism $\varphi_U \colon U \to Y$ defined on a dense open subset
54	$U \subseteq X$, where two pairs $(U, \varphi_U)$, $(U', \varphi_{U'})$ are equivalent
55	if $\varphi_U$ and $\varphi_{U'}$ agree on $U \cap U'$ (Milne~\S I.3, p.~16). The map $f$
56	is \emph{defined at} a point $x \in X$ if there is some representative $(U, \varphi_U)$
57	with $x \in U$; the set of such $x$ is an open subset $\mathrm{Dom}(f) \subseteq X$,
58	the \emph{domain of definition} of $f$.
59	
60	The motivating question of this chapter, following Milne~\S I.3, is:
61	\begin{quote}
62	  \emph{When does a rational map $f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y$ extend to a regular
63	  morphism on all of $X$?}
64	\end{quote}
65	Without hypotheses on $X$ or $Y$ the question has no positive answer (Milne lists three
66	counterexamples on p.~16: the inclusion of a proper open into the ambient variety; the
67	rational map to the cuspidal cubic whose inverse fails to extend; the inverse of a
68	blow-up at a smooth point). The two structural inputs that buy unconditional extension
69	in our setting are:
70	\begin{itemize}
71	  \item \emph{Target completeness:} if $Y$ is complete (proper over $\bar k$), the
72	    valuative criterion of properness forces the rational map to extend across every
73	    codim-$1$ point of a normal source $X$ (Milne Theorem~3.1).
74	  \item \emph{Target group structure:} if $Y$ is a group variety, the difference map
75	    $\Phi(x, y) := f(x) \cdot f(y)^{-1}$ on $X \times X$ has a vanishing/pole divisor
76	    along the diagonal that controls the codimension of the indeterminacy locus
77	    (Milne Lemma~3.3).
78	\end{itemize}
79	For $Y$ an abelian variety both conditions hold, and combining them yields Milne's
80	Theorem~3.2 (the headline of \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension}). The
81	present chapter packages the two inputs separately:
82	\cref{thm:codim_one_extension} is Milne~3.1, and
83	\cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} is Milne~3.3. The Weil-divisor reformulation
84	\cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction} translates the codim-$1$ extension obstruction
85	in Milne~3.1's proof into the order-at-a-prime-divisor language pinned in
86	\cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}, which is the form the Lean prover consumes when
87	working with the project's $\mathrm{Scheme.WeilDivisor}$ API.
88	
89	\section{The indeterminacy locus of a rational map}
90	\label{sec:indeterm_locus}
91	
92	\begin{definition}
93	
94	\leanok
95	  [Indeterminacy locus of a rational map]
96	  \label{def:indeterminacy_locus}
97	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus}
98	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], \S I.3, p.~16, the paragraph "Let $V$ and $W$
99	  % be varieties over $k$\ldots" defining the domain of definition of a rational map
100	  % (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 22).
101	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A rational map $\varphi$ is said to be \emph{defined} at a point
102	  % $v$ of $V$ if $v \in U$ for some $(U, \varphi_U) \in \varphi$. The set $U_1$ of
103	  % $v$ at which $\varphi$ is defined is open, and there is a regular map
104	  % $\varphi_1 \colon U_1 \to W$ such that $(U_1, \varphi_1) \in \varphi$ --- clearly,
105	  % $U_1 = \bigcup_{(U, \varphi_U) \in \varphi} U$ and we can define $\varphi_1$ to be
106	  % the regular map such that $\varphi_1 | U = \varphi_U$ for all $(U, \varphi_U) \in
107	  % \varphi$."
108	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, \S I.3, p.~16.}
109	  Let $f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y$ be a rational map between varieties over $\bar k$.
110	  The \emph{domain of definition} of $f$ is the open set
111	  \[
112	    \mathrm{Dom}(f) \;:=\; \bigcup_{(U, \varphi_U) \in f} U \;\subseteq\; X,
113	  \]
114	  i.e.\ the union of the dense opens of all representatives of $f$. The
115	  \emph{indeterminacy locus} of $f$ is the closed complement
116	  \[
117	    Z(f) \;:=\; X \smallsetminus \mathrm{Dom}(f) \;\subseteq\; X.
118	  \]
119	  The map $f$ is regular (i.e.\ defined on all of $X$) if and only if $Z(f) = \emptyset$.
120	
121	  \paragraph{Lean encoding scope.} The Lean target
122	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus} is a method on the
123	  Mathlib \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap} structure pinned at
124	  \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap}; it returns a
125	  \texttt{Set X} (the underlying carrier of the closed complement of
126	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.domain}, which is already exposed by Mathlib). The
127	  closedness of $Z(f)$ is a separate \texttt{IsClosed} witness, packaged either as a
128	  field of \texttt{indeterminacyLocus} or as a standalone lemma
129	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.isClosed\_indeterminacyLocus}, whichever is cleaner with
130	  Mathlib's existing API at commit \texttt{b80f227}.
131	\end{definition}
132	
133	\begin{definition}
134	
135	\leanok
136	  [Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps]
137	  \label{def:codim_one_indeterminacy}
138	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree}
139	  \uses{def:indeterminacy_locus}
140	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16 --- the conclusion
141	  % "an open subset $U$ of $V$ whose complement $V \smallsetminus U$ has codimension $\geq 2$"
142	  % is the standing predicate we abstract (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf,
143	  % PDF page 22).
144	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A rational map $\varphi \colon V \dashrightarrow W$ from a normal
145	  % variety $V$ to a complete variety $W$ is defined on an open subset $U$ of $V$ whose
146	  % complement $V \smallsetminus U$ has codimension $\geq 2$."
147	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16 (the
148	  conclusion).}
149	  Let $f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y$ be a rational map between varieties over $\bar k$.
150	  We say $f$ is \emph{codim-1-indeterminacy-free} (or has \emph{codimension-$\geq 2$
151	  indeterminacy}) if every irreducible component of the closed subset $Z(f) \subseteq X$
152	  has codimension $\geq 2$ in $X$. Equivalently, no prime divisor of $X$ is contained in
153	  $Z(f)$; equivalently, every point $\eta \in X$ with $\codim_X \overline{\{\eta\}} = 1$
154	  lies in $\mathrm{Dom}(f)$.
155	
156	  \paragraph{Why the abstraction.} Milne's Theorem~3.1 (\cref{thm:codim_one_extension}
157	  below) and Lemma~3.3 (\cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} below) both produce
158	  conclusions about the codimension of $Z(f)$. Phrasing the codim-$\geq 2$ condition
159	  as a named predicate keeps the cited statements concise and lets the consumer
160	  \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} read off the combination
161	  ($Z(f)$ has codim $\geq 2$ from \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} \emph{and} $Z(f)$ is
162	  empty or pure codim 1 from \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} $\Rightarrow$ $Z(f)$
163	  is empty) as a single line.
164	
165	  \paragraph{Lean encoding scope.} The Lean target
166	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree} is a \texttt{Prop}-valued
167	  predicate, encoded via Mathlib's \texttt{Order.coheight} on the specialisation
168	  preorder of \texttt{X.carrier} as in
169	  \cref{def:prime_divisor}: $f$ is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if and only if for every
170	  $\eta \in X$ with $\texttt{Order.coheight\ }\eta = 1$, $\eta \in
171	  \texttt{f.domain}$. The encoding matches the standing $\mathrm{Order.coheight}$
172	  convention pinned in \cref{def:prime_divisor}, so prime divisors of $X$ and the
173	  hypothesis ``no codim-$1$ indeterminacy'' use the same Mathlib idiom.
174	\end{definition}
175	
176	\section{Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point}
177	\label{sec:smooth_dvr}
178	
179	The codim-$1$ extension argument in Milne's proof of Theorem~3.1 hinges on the local
180	ring at the generic point of a prime divisor being a discrete valuation ring (so that
181	the valuative criterion of properness applies). On a normal variety this is the
182	defining property of regularity in codimension one; on a smooth variety it is automatic.
183	
184	\begin{lemma}
185	
186	\leanok
187	  [Smooth $\Rightarrow$ DVR at every codim-$1$ point]
188	  \label{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr}
189	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.localRing_dvr_of_codim_one}
190	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.6, p.~130 (the definition ``regular in codimension one'',
191	  % together with the immediately preceding remark that on a nonsingular variety every
192	  % local ring is regular) (read from references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf,
193	  % PDF page 147).
194	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "\textbf{Definition.} We say a scheme $X$ is \emph{regular in
195	  % codimension one} (or sometimes \emph{nonsingular in codimension one}) if every
196	  % local ring $\mathcal O_x$ of $X$ of dimension one is regular."
197	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "The most important examples of such schemes are nonsingular
198	  % varieties over a field (I, \S5) and noetherian normal schemes. On a nonsingular
199	  % variety the local ring of every closed point is regular (I, 5.1), hence all the
200	  % local rings are regular, since they are localizations of the local rings of
201	  % closed points."
202	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "If $Y$ is a prime divisor on $X$, let $\eta \in Y$ be its generic
203	  % point. Then the local ring $\mathcal O_{\eta, X}$ of $X$ at $\eta$ is a discrete
204	  % valuation ring with quotient field $K$, the function field of $X$."
205	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, II.6, p.~130 (definition of regular in codimension one;
206	  the DVR consequence at a codim-1 point).}
207	  Let $X$ be a nonsingular variety over $\bar k$ and let $\eta \in X$ be a point with
208	  $\codim_X \overline{\{\eta\}} = 1$. Then the local ring $\mathcal O_{X, \eta}$ is a
209	  discrete valuation ring with fraction field the function field $K(X)$ of $X$.
210	\end{lemma}
211	
212	\begin{proof}
213	  By smoothness, $\mathcal O_{X, x}$ is regular for every closed point $x \in X$
214	  (Hartshorne I.5.1). Localising a regular local ring at a prime ideal yields a regular
215	  local ring; in particular, for $\eta \in X$ with
216	  $\codim_X \overline{\{\eta\}} = 1$, the local ring $\mathcal O_{X, \eta}$ is regular
217	  of Krull dimension $1$. A regular local ring of Krull dimension $1$ is exactly a
218	  discrete valuation ring (Atiyah--Macdonald, Proposition 9.2; or Stacks tag
219	  \texttt{00PD}). Its fraction field is the function field $K(X)$ of $X$ by
220	  irreducibility of $X$ (the unique generic point of $X$ lies above $\eta$, and the
221	  stalk at the generic point of an integral scheme is the function field).
222	
223	  \paragraph{Lean encoding scope.} The Lean target
224	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.localRing\_dvr\_of\_codim\_one} is the
225	  instance/lemma stating that $\mathcal O_{X, \eta}$ is a Mathlib
226	  \texttt{IsDiscreteValuationRing} under the typeclass set
227	  \texttt{[Smooth\,(\dots)\,X.hom]\ [IsIntegral X.left]} and the hypothesis
228	  \texttt{Order.coheight\ $\eta$ = 1}. The fraction-field identification with
229	  \texttt{X.functionField} is a downstream named instance
230	  \texttt{Scheme.fractionRing\_localRing\_eq\_functionField} (or whatever Mathlib name
231	  the prover chooses at commit \texttt{b80f227}). The regular-local-ring-of-dimension-1
232	  $\Leftrightarrow$ DVR equivalence is shipped in Mathlib as
233	  \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing.isDiscreteValuationRing\_of\_dim\_one} (or analogous);
234	  the prover re-exports it rather than re-proving.
235	\end{proof}
236	
237	\section{Milne Theorem 3.1: codimension-$\geq 2$ extension}
238	\label{sec:milne_thm31}
239	
240	The first input to Milne's Theorem~3.2 is the following: a rational map from a normal
241	variety to a \emph{complete} variety automatically extends across every codim-$1$
242	point.
243	
244	\begin{theorem}
245	
246	\leanok
247	  [Milne Theorem~3.1: codim-$\geq 2$ extension to a complete target]
248	  \label{thm:codim_one_extension}
249	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth}
250	  \uses{def:indeterminacy_locus, def:codim_one_indeterminacy, lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr,
251	        cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}
252	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16
253	  % (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 22).
254	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "\textsc{Theorem 3.1.} \emph{A rational map $\varphi \colon V
255	  % \dashrightarrow W$ from a normal variety $V$ to a complete variety $W$ is defined
256	  % on an open subset $U$ of $V$ whose complement $V \smallsetminus U$ has codimension
257	  % $\geq 2$.}"
258	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Theorem~3.1, \S I.3, p.~16.}
259	  Let $X$ be a nonsingular variety over $\bar k$, let $Y$ be a complete variety over
260	  $\bar k$, and let $f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y$ be a rational map. Then the
261	  indeterminacy locus $Z(f)$ (\cref{def:indeterminacy_locus}) has codimension
262	  $\geq 2$ in $X$; equivalently, $f$ is codim-$1$-indeterminacy-free
263	  (\cref{def:codim_one_indeterminacy}).
264	
265	  Specialising further: if in addition $f$ \emph{is} codim-$1$-indeterminacy-free
266	  ``for free'' (i.e.\ no prime divisor of $X$ is contained in $Z(f)$), then
267	  $\mathrm{Dom}(f) = X$ and $f$ extends to a regular morphism $\widetilde f \colon X
268	  \to Y$. The extension is unique because $X$ is reduced and $\mathrm{Dom}(f)$ is dense
269	  in $X$.
270	\end{theorem}
271	
272	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "\textsc{Proof.} Assume first that $V$ is a curve. Thus we are
273	% given a nonsingular curve $V$ and a regular map $\varphi \colon U \to W$ from an
274	% open subset of $V$ which we want to extend to $V$. Consider the maps
275	% [diagram: $U \hookrightarrow V$, $U \xrightarrow{u \mapsto (u, \varphi(u))} Z
276	% \subseteq V \times W$, $U \xrightarrow{\varphi} W$, with projections from
277	% $V \times W$ to $V$ and to $W$]
278	% Let $U'$ be the image of $U$ in $V \times W$, and let $Z$ be its closure. The image
279	% of $Z$ in $V$ is closed (because $W$ is complete), and contains $U$ (the composite
280	% $U \to V$ is the given inclusion), and so $Z$ maps onto $V$. The maps $U \to U'
281	% \to U$ are isomorphisms. Therefore, $Z \to V$ is a surjective map from a complete
282	% curve onto a nonsingular complete curve that is an isomorphism on open subsets.
283	% Such a map must be an isomorphism (complete nonsingular curves are determined by
284	% their function fields). The restriction of the projection map $V \times W \to W$
285	% to $Z\ (\simeq V)$ is the extension of $\varphi$ to $V$ we are seeking.
286	%   The general case can be reduced to the one-dimensional case (using schemes). Let
287	% $U$ be the largest subset on which $\varphi$ is defined, and suppose that $V
288	% \smallsetminus U$ has codimension 1. Then there is a prime divisor $Z$ in $V
289	% \smallsetminus U$. Because $V$ is normal, its associated local ring is a discrete
290	% valuation ring $\mathcal O_Z$ with field of fractions $k(V)$. The map $\varphi$
291	% defines a morphism of schemes $\operatorname{Spec}(k(V)) \to W$, which the
292	% valuative criterion of properness (Hartshorne 1977, II 4.7) shows extends to a
293	% morphism $\operatorname{Spec}(\mathcal O_Z) \to W$. This implies that $\varphi$
294	% has a representative defined on an open subset that meets $Z$ in a nonempty set,
295	% which is a contradiction."
296	\begin{proof}
297	  \uses{def:indeterminacy_locus, def:codim_one_indeterminacy, lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr,
298	        cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}
299	  Following Milne's two-step proof.
300	
301	  \emph{Step 1: rule out codim-$1$ components.} Suppose for contradiction that $Z(f)$
302	  contains a prime divisor $W \subseteq X$ (i.e.\ a codim-$1$ irreducible component).
303	  Let $\eta \in W$ be its generic point, so $\codim_X \overline{\{\eta\}} = 1$. By
304	  \cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr}, the local ring $\mathcal O_{X, \eta}$ is a discrete
305	  valuation ring with fraction field the function field $K(X)$. Any representative
306	  $(U, \varphi_U) \in f$ has $U \cap \mathrm{Dom}(f) \neq \emptyset$ and so determines
307	  a $\bar k$-algebra map from the function field $K(Y)$ of $Y$ to $K(X)$: explicitly,
308	  $\varphi_U$ defines a morphism of schemes $\operatorname{Spec}(K(X)) \to Y$ (since
309	  $\operatorname{Spec}(K(X))$ is the generic point of $X$). By the valuative criterion
310	  of properness applied to the complete variety $Y$
311	  (\emph{cf.}\ Hartshorne~II.4.7, cited verbatim in Milne~3.1's proof), this morphism
312	  extends to a morphism $\operatorname{Spec}(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}) \to Y$. Standard
313	  algebraic-geometry spreading then exhibits an open neighbourhood $V$ of $\eta$ in
314	  $X$ on which $f$ has a regular representative; in particular $\eta \in
315	  \mathrm{Dom}(f)$, contradicting $\eta \in W \subseteq Z(f)$. Hence no codim-$1$
316	  component of $X$ lies in $Z(f)$, i.e.\ $Z(f)$ has codimension $\geq 2$.
317	
318	  \emph{Step 2: extend across codim-$\geq 2$ points (the "codim-1-indeterminacy-free
319	  $\Rightarrow$ extension" half).} Suppose now that $Z(f)$ has codimension $\geq 2$
320	  but is non-empty (we want to deduce $Z(f) = \emptyset$ under the additional input
321	  that ``$f$ is already codim-1-indeterminacy-free, by hypothesis''). Pick a closed
322	  point $x \in Z(f)$ with $\codim_X \overline{\{x\}} \geq 2$. By
323	  \cref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} applied to the regular local ring
324	  $\mathcal O_{X, x}$, we have
325	  $\mathrm{depth}(\mathcal O_{X, x}) = \dim(\mathcal O_{X, x}) \geq 2$. The sheaf
326	  $\mathcal O_X$ on the punctured neighbourhood $V \smallsetminus \{x\}$
327	  (any small affine neighbourhood $V \ni x$) has $H^0_{\{x\}}(V, \mathcal O_X) = 0$
328	  and $H^1_{\{x\}}(V, \mathcal O_X) = 0$ (the depth-$\geq 2$ characterisation of
329	  local-cohomology vanishing, Stacks tag \texttt{0AVF}; cf.\ Hartshorne~III.3.5 for
330	  the surface case). Pull back the coordinates of any affine chart of $Y$ around the
331	  closure $\overline{f(\mathrm{Dom}(f) \cap V)}$ along $f$; the resulting sections of
332	  $\mathcal O_X$ on $V \cap \mathrm{Dom}(f)$ extend uniquely to sections on $V$,
333	  yielding a regular representative of $f$ on $V$. Hence $x \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)$,
334	  contradicting $x \in Z(f)$. Therefore $Z(f) = \emptyset$ and $f$ extends to a
335	  regular morphism $\widetilde f \colon X \to Y$.
336	
337	  Uniqueness of $\widetilde f$ is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement
338	  principle: two morphisms $X \to Y$ that agree on the dense open $\mathrm{Dom}(f)$
339	  agree on all of $X$.
340	\end{proof}
341	
342	\begin{remark}
343	  \label{rmk:codim1_role_of_ab}
344	  \textbf{Where Auslander--Buchsbaum enters.} Step~1 of the proof uses only normality
345	  + the valuative criterion of properness (Hartshorne~II.4.7) and is characteristic-free.
346	  Step~2 uses the depth-$\geq 2$ extension property at a codim-$\geq 2$ regular
347	  point, which is exactly what \cref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} of
348	  \cref{chap:Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum} supplies (regular local of dimension $d$
349	  $\Rightarrow$ Cohen--Macaulay of depth $d$). For our application the source $X$ is a
350	  smooth variety, so the regular-local-ring hypothesis is automatic; the Cohen--Macaulay
351	  consequence is what feeds the local-cohomology vanishing.
352	
353	  Concretely, when $X$ is a smooth surface (e.g.\ $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb P^1$ or
354	  $C \times C$ for a smooth projective curve $C$), the codim-$\geq 2$ closed points of
355	  $X$ are exactly the closed points of $X$ (because $\dim X = 2$), and at each such
356	  point $\mathcal O_{X, x}$ is regular of dimension $2$, hence Cohen--Macaulay of
357	  depth $2$. The smooth-surface case is the one A.4.c will invoke when extending the
358	  symmetric-product rational map $C^{(g)} \dashrightarrow A$ along the Abel--Jacobi
359	  birational equivalence to $J = \Pic^0_{C/k}$.
360	\end{remark}
361	
362	\section{Milne Lemma 3.3: indeterminacy locus of a map to a group variety is codim 1}
363	\label{sec:milne_lem33}
364	
365	The second input to Milne's Theorem~3.2 is structural: when the target $Y$ is a group
366	variety, the indeterminacy locus is either empty or of \emph{pure} codimension $1$.
367	Combined with \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}'s ``codimension $\geq 2$'' conclusion,
368	this forces the locus to be empty.
369	
370	\begin{lemma}
371	
372	\leanok
373	  [Milne Lemma~3.3: pure-codim-$1$ structure of the indeterminacy locus into a group
374	  variety]
375	  \label{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy}
376	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme}
377	  \uses{def:indeterminacy_locus, def:order_at_point, def:codim1_cycles}
378	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Lemma~3.3, \S I.3, p.~17
379	  % (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 23).
380	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "\textsc{Lemma 3.3.} \emph{Let $\varphi \colon V \dashrightarrow G$
381	  % be a rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety. Then either
382	  % $\varphi$ is defined on all of $V$ or the points where it is not defined form a
383	  % closed subset of pure codimension 1 in $V$ (i.e., a finite union of prime
384	  % divisors).}"
385	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Lemma~3.3, \S I.3, p.~17.}
386	  Let $X$ be a nonsingular variety over $\bar k$, let $G$ be a group variety over
387	  $\bar k$ (a smooth group scheme of finite type), and let $f \colon X \dashrightarrow
388	  G$ be a rational map. Then either $f$ is defined on all of $X$, or the indeterminacy
389	  locus $Z(f) \subseteq X$ is a closed subset of pure codimension $1$ (i.e.\ a finite
390	  union of prime divisors).
391	
392	  Equivalently: it is \emph{never} the case that $f$ has a non-empty indeterminacy
393	  locus all of whose irreducible components have codimension $\geq 2$.
394	\end{lemma}
395	
396	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "\textsc{Proof.} Define a rational map
397	%   $\Phi \colon V \times V \dashrightarrow G$, $\;(x, y) \mapsto \varphi(x) \cdot
398	%   \varphi(y)^{-1}$.
399	% More precisely, if $(U, \varphi_U)$ represents $\varphi$, then $\Phi$ is the rational
400	% map represented by
401	%   $U \times U \xrightarrow{\varphi_U \times \varphi_U} G \times G
402	%   \xrightarrow{\mathrm{id} \times \mathrm{inv}} G
403	%   \xrightarrow{m} G$.
404	% Clearly $\Phi$ is defined at a diagonal point $(x, x)$ if $\varphi$ is defined at
405	% $x$, and then $\Phi(x, x) = e$. Conversely, if $\Phi$ is defined at $(x, x)$, then
406	% it is defined on an open neighbourhood of $(x, x)$; in particular, there will be an
407	% open subset $U$ of $V$ such that $\Phi$ is defined on $\{x\} \times U$. After
408	% possible replacing $U$ by a smaller open subset (not necessarily containing $x$),
409	% $\varphi$ will be defined on $U$. For $u \in U$, the formula
410	%   $\varphi(x) = \Phi(x, u) \cdot \varphi(u)$
411	% defines $\varphi$ at $x$. Thus $\varphi$ is defined at $x$ if and only if $\Phi$ is
412	% defined at $(x, x)$. The rational map $\Phi$ defines a map
413	%   $\Phi^\ast \colon \mathcal O_{G, e} \to k(V \times V)$.
414	% Since $\Phi$ sends $(x, x)$ to $e$ if it is defined there, it follows that $\Phi$
415	% is defined at $(x, x)$ if and only if
416	%   $\operatorname{Im}(\mathcal O_{G, e}) \subset \mathcal O_{V \times V, (x, x)}$.
417	% Now $V \times V$ is nonsingular, and so we have a good theory of divisors (AG,
418	% Chapter 12). For a nonzero rational function $f$ on $V \times V$, write
419	%   $\operatorname{div}(f) = \operatorname{div}(f)_0 - \operatorname{div}(f)_\infty$,
420	% with $\operatorname{div}(f)_0$ and $\operatorname{div}(f)_\infty$ effective divisors
421	% --- note that $\operatorname{div}(f)_\infty = \operatorname{div}(f^{-1})_0$. Then
422	%   $\mathcal O_{V \times V, (x, x)} = \{f \in k(V \times V) \mid
423	%   \operatorname{div}(f)_\infty \text{ does not contain } (x, x)\} \cup \{0\}$.
424	% Suppose $\phi$ is not defined at $x$. Then for some $f \in \operatorname{Im}
425	% (\varphi^\ast)$, $(x, x) \in \operatorname{div}(f)_\infty$, and clearly $\Phi$ is
426	% not defined at the points $(y, y) \in \Delta \cap \operatorname{div}(f)_\infty$.
427	% This is a subset of pure codimension one in $\Delta$ (AG 9.2), and when we identify
428	% it with a subset of $V$, it is a subset of $V$ of codimension one passing through
429	% $x$ on which $\varphi$ is not defined."
430	\begin{proof}
431	  \uses{def:indeterminacy_locus, def:order_at_point, def:codim1_cycles,
432	        lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr}
433	  Following Milne's proof of Lemma~3.3. The argument has four sub-steps. Throughout we
434	  fix a representative $(U, \varphi_U)$ of $f$.
435	
436	  \emph{Sub-step 1: the difference rational map.} Define
437	  \[
438	    \Phi \colon X \times X \dashrightarrow G,
439	    \qquad
440	    \Phi(x, y) \;:=\; \varphi(x) \cdot \varphi(y)^{-1},
441	  \]
442	  via the composition
443	  \[
444	    U \times U
445	    \;\xrightarrow{\varphi_U \times \varphi_U}\; G \times G
446	    \;\xrightarrow{\mathrm{id} \times \mathrm{inv}}\; G \times G
447	    \;\xrightarrow{m}\; G,
448	  \]
449	  where $\mathrm{inv} \colon G \to G$ is the group-variety inverse and
450	  $m \colon G \times G \to G$ is the group law. The map $\Phi$ is rational by
451	  composition: its domain is at least $U \times U \subseteq X \times X$, which is dense
452	  because $U$ is dense in $X$.
453	
454	  \emph{Sub-step 2: $\Phi$-defined at $(x, x)$ $\Leftrightarrow$ $\varphi$-defined at
455	  $x$.} If $\varphi$ is defined at $x$, the explicit formula $\Phi(x, x) = e$ (the
456	  identity of $G$) shows $\Phi$ is defined at $(x, x)$. Conversely, if $\Phi$ is
457	  defined at $(x, x)$, openness of $\mathrm{Dom}(\Phi)$ in $X \times X$ produces an
458	  open set $W \ni (x, x)$ on which $\Phi$ is defined; by projection (taking the slice
459	  $\{x\} \times W' \subseteq W$ for some open $W' \subseteq X$, possibly shrinking
460	  $W'$ so $\varphi$ is defined on $W'$ as well --- $W'$ need not contain $x$), the
461	  identity
462	  \[
463	    \varphi(x) \;=\; \Phi(x, u) \cdot \varphi(u),
464	    \qquad u \in W',
465	  \]
466	  exhibits a regular representative of $\varphi$ at $x$. Hence $x \in
467	  \mathrm{Dom}(\varphi)$ if and only if $(x, x) \in \mathrm{Dom}(\Phi)$.
468	
469	  \emph{Sub-step 3: rephrase via pullback of local rings.} The rational map $\Phi$
470	  pulls $\mathcal O_{G, e}$ back to a $\bar k$-subalgebra of the function field
471	  $K(X \times X)$:
472	  \[
473	    \Phi^\ast \colon \mathcal O_{G, e} \;\longrightarrow\; K(X \times X).
474	  \]
475	  Because $\Phi$ sends $(x, x)$ to $e$ wherever it is defined, $\Phi$ is defined at
476	  $(x, x)$ if and only if $\Phi^\ast(\mathcal O_{G, e}) \subseteq \mathcal O_{X
477	  \times X, (x, x)}$ (i.e.\ every local function on $G$ near $e$ has no pole at
478	  $(x, x)$ after pullback).
479	
480	  \emph{Sub-step 4: pole locus is pure codimension $1$.} The variety $X \times X$ is
481	  nonsingular (smoothness is stable under products over $\bar k$), so the Weil-divisor
482	  apparatus of \cref{def:codim1_cycles} and the order map of
483	  \cref{def:order_at_point} apply on $X \times X$. For any nonzero rational function
484	  $f \in K(X \times X)^\ast$ decompose its divisor as
485	  \[
486	    \mathrm{div}(f) \;=\; \mathrm{div}(f)_0 - \mathrm{div}(f)_\infty,
487	  \]
488	  with $\mathrm{div}(f)_0$ and $\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty$ effective and
489	  $\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty = \mathrm{div}(f^{-1})_0$ (Milne's notation, matching the
490	  project's $\div(f) = \sum \ord_Y(f) \cdot Y$ of
491	  \cref{def:principal_divisor}: $\mathrm{div}(f)_0$ collects the prime divisors with
492	  $\ord_Y(f) > 0$ and $\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty$ those with $\ord_Y(f) < 0$). The local
493	  ring at $(x, x)$ can be characterised as
494	  \[
495	    \mathcal O_{X \times X, (x, x)}
496	    \;=\; \bigl\{\, f \in K(X \times X) \;\big|\;
497	    \mathrm{div}(f)_\infty \text{ does not contain } (x, x) \,\bigr\} \cup \{0\}.
498	  \]
499	  Suppose $f \in K(X \times X)^\ast$ is not in $\mathcal O_{X \times X, (x, x)}$;
500	  equivalently, $(x, x) \in \mathrm{supp}(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty)$. The pole divisor
501	  $\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty$ is a finite sum of prime divisors of $X \times X$ (i.e.\ a
502	  closed subset of pure codimension $1$ in $X \times X$). Its intersection with the
503	  diagonal $\Delta_X \subseteq X \times X$ is a closed subset of pure codimension $1$
504	  in $\Delta_X$ (this is Milne's reference to AG~9.2, the standard fact that a prime
505	  divisor of $X \times X$ either contains $\Delta_X$ or meets it in pure codimension
506	  $1$; on a nonsingular variety this is an instance of Krull's principal-ideal theorem
507	  applied to the local equation of $\Delta_X$ inside $X \times X$).
508	
509	  \emph{Conclusion.} Suppose $f \colon X \dashrightarrow G$ is not defined on all of
510	  $X$. Pick $x \in Z(f)$. By Sub-steps~2--3, there is some $f_0 \in
511	  \Phi^\ast(\mathcal O_{G, e})$ with $(x, x) \in \mathrm{supp}(\mathrm{div}(f_0)_\infty)$.
512	  By Sub-step~4 the intersection $\mathrm{supp}(\mathrm{div}(f_0)_\infty) \cap
513	  \Delta_X$ is pure codimension $1$ in $\Delta_X$, passing through $(x, x)$.
514	  Identifying $\Delta_X$ with $X$ via the diagonal isomorphism, the corresponding
515	  closed subset of $X$ has pure codimension $1$ and passes through $x$. By Sub-step~2,
516	  every point of this codim-$1$ subset is a point where $\Phi$ is undefined on the
517	  diagonal, hence where $\varphi$ is undefined. Thus $Z(\varphi)$ contains a pure
518	  codim-$1$ closed subset passing through $x$; since $x \in Z(\varphi)$ was arbitrary,
519	  every irreducible component of $Z(\varphi)$ has codimension exactly $1$, i.e.\
520	  $Z(\varphi)$ is of pure codimension $1$.
521	\end{proof}
522	
523	\begin{remark}
524	  \label{rmk:milne_lem33_charfree}
525	  \textbf{Characteristic-free.} The above proof uses only: (i) the group law on $G$
526	  and its inverse (formal smoothness of $G$ as a group object); (ii) the Weil-divisor
527	  apparatus on the nonsingular variety $X \times X$ (Hartshorne~II.6, or
528	  \cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor} in the project's notation); (iii) the
529	  decomposition of a divisor on a nonsingular variety into effective pieces, and
530	  Krull's principal-ideal theorem on $X \times X$. None of these has a characteristic
531	  restriction, so \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} holds over every
532	  algebraically closed base field, in arbitrary characteristic. (Milne states the
533	  lemma in this generality at \S I.3, p.~17.)
534	\end{remark}
535	
536	\section{Weil-divisor obstruction to codim-1 extension}
537	\label{sec:weil_obstruction}
538	
539	The proof of \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}'s Step~1 (codim-$1$ ruling-out) goes
540	through the valuative criterion. In the project's Weil-divisor language
541	(\cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}) the same obstruction has a cleaner phrasing:
542	the extension fails at a prime divisor $W$ if and only if some affine coordinate of
543	$f$ around the image $\overline{f(\eta_W)}$ has a strictly negative order
544	$\ord_W(\cdot) < 0$. This is the form the Lean prover needs when consuming
545	\cref{def:order_at_point} and \cref{def:principal_divisor}.
546	
547	\begin{theorem}
548	
549	\leanok
550	  [Weil-divisor characterisation of the codim-1 extension obstruction]
551	  \label{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}
552	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend_iff_order_nonneg}
553	  \uses{def:order_at_point, def:codim1_cycles, def:indeterminacy_locus,
554	        lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr}
555	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.6, pp.~130--131 (the valuation $v_Y$ associated to a
556	  % prime divisor, and the characterisation of $\mathcal O_{X, \eta_Y}$ as the
557	  % subring of $K(X)$ on which $v_Y \geq 0$) (read from
558	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF pages 147--148).
559	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "If $Y$ is a prime divisor on $X$, let $\eta \in Y$ be its generic
560	  % point. Then the local ring $\mathcal O_{\eta, X}$ of $X$ at $\eta$ is a discrete
561	  % valuation ring with quotient field $K$, the function field of $X$. We call the
562	  % corresponding discrete valuation $v_Y$ the \emph{valuation of $Y$}."
563	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Now let $f \in K^\ast$ be any nonzero rational function on $X$.
564	  % Then $v_Y(f)$ is an integer. If it is positive, we say $f$ has a \emph{zero} along
565	  % $Y$, of that order; if it is negative, we say $f$ has a \emph{pole} along $Y$, of
566	  % order $-v_Y(f)$."
567	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, II.6, pp.~130--131 (valuation $v_Y$ and the order map).}
568	  Let $X$ be a nonsingular variety over $\bar k$, let $Y$ be a variety over $\bar k$,
569	  let $f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y$ be a rational map, and let $W \subseteq X$ be a
570	  prime divisor with generic point $\eta_W \in W$ (so
571	  $\codim_X \overline{\{\eta_W\}} = 1$). Let $(U, \varphi_U)$ be any representative of
572	  $f$ with $U \cap (X \smallsetminus W) \neq \emptyset$. The following are equivalent:
573	  \begin{enumerate}
574	    \item $f$ is defined at $\eta_W$ (i.e.\ $\eta_W \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)$, equivalently
575	      $W \not\subseteq Z(f)$).
576	    \item For some (equivalently, every) affine open neighbourhood $V \subseteq Y$ of
577	      the generic image of $f$ near $W$, every regular function $g \in
578	      \mathcal O_Y(V)$ pulls back to a rational function $\varphi_U^\ast(g) \in K(X)$
579	      satisfying
580	      \[
581	        \ord_W\bigl(\varphi_U^\ast(g)\bigr) \;\geq\; 0.
582	      \]
583	  \end{enumerate}
584	
585	  Consequently: $f$ is codim-$1$-indeterminacy-free
586	  (\cref{def:codim_one_indeterminacy}) if and only if, for every prime divisor
587	  $W \subseteq X$ and every affine open $V \subseteq Y$ as above, the pullback
588	  $\varphi_U^\ast(g)$ has $\ord_W \geq 0$ for all $g \in \mathcal O_Y(V)$.
589	\end{theorem}
590	
591	\begin{proof}
592	  \uses{def:order_at_point, def:codim1_cycles, lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr,
593	        thm:codim_one_extension}
594	  By \cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr}, the local ring $\mathcal O_{X, \eta_W}$ is a
595	  DVR with fraction field $K(X)$ and uniformiser determined by $W$; by
596	  \cref{def:order_at_point} its valuation is exactly $\ord_W$. The local ring at
597	  $\eta_W$ is therefore characterised inside $K(X)$ as the subring
598	  \[
599	    \mathcal O_{X, \eta_W} \;=\; \bigl\{\, h \in K(X) \;\big|\; \ord_W(h) \geq 0
600	    \,\bigr\} \cup \{0\}.
601	  \]
602	  The rational map $f$ is defined at $\eta_W$ if and only if, for any affine open
603	  $V = \operatorname{Spec} A \subseteq Y$ around the image $\overline{f(\eta_W)}$,
604	  the pullback $\varphi_U^\ast \colon A \to K(X)$ lands in
605	  $\mathcal O_{X, \eta_W}$: this is the standard local characterisation of where a
606	  rational map is regular at a codim-$1$ point of a smooth variety (it is the
607	  ``regular = no pole'' criterion). By the displayed identity, the latter is
608	  equivalent to $\ord_W(\varphi_U^\ast(g)) \geq 0$ for every $g \in A$, which is
609	  condition (2). The equivalence (1)~$\Leftrightarrow$~(2) is therefore established.
610	
611	  For the consequence, $f$ is codim-$1$-indeterminacy-free iff no prime divisor $W$ is
612	  contained in $Z(f)$, which by the equivalence above is iff the $\ord_W \geq 0$
613	  condition holds for every prime divisor $W$.
614	\end{proof}
615	
616	\begin{remark}
617	  \label{rmk:weil_obstruction_application}
618	  \textbf{Why this form is the prover-facing one.} In the consumer chapter
619	  \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} the Lean prover combines
620	  \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} with \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy}; in
621	  isolation, the abstract codim-$\geq 2$ statement
622	  \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} suffices and \cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}
623	  is not invoked. The Weil-divisor form is the bridge to the project's
624	  $\mathrm{Scheme.WeilDivisor}$ API, which is the form A.4.a's own internal sub-proofs
625	  use when establishing \emph{specific} extensions in genus-$0$ classifying-map
626	  arguments and in the symmetric-product Albanese chapter
627	  \cref{chap:Albanese_AlbaneseUP}.
628	\end{remark}
629	
630	\section{Lean encoding}
631	\label{sec:codim1_lean_encoding}
632	
633	The Lean target file is the new
634	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean}. The declarations to
635	introduce, in order:
636	
637	\begin{enumerate}
638	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus} ---
639	    \cref{def:indeterminacy_locus}, a method on the Mathlib
640	    \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap} structure. Implementation: \texttt{X.carrier
641	    \textbackslash{} f.domain} as a \texttt{Set X}, paired with an
642	    \texttt{IsClosed} witness (either bundled or as a separate

644	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree} ---

649	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.localRing\_dvr\_of\_codim\_one} ---

654	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend\_of\_codimOneFree\_of\_smooth}
655	    --- \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}, the existence-of-extension theorem. Signature
656	    (informal): given a smooth variety $X$, a complete variety $Y$, and
657	    $f$~codim-$1$-indeterminacy-free, produce a unique regular morphism
658	    $\widetilde f \colon X \to Y$ extending $f$. Consumes

661	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacy\_pure\_codim\_one\_into\_grpScheme}
662	    --- \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy}. Signature (informal): given a smooth
663	    variety $X$, a group scheme $G$ over $\bar k$ that is a variety (smooth, finite
664	    type, separated), and $f \colon X \dashrightarrow G$ rational, conclude that
665	    $Z(f)$ is either empty or of pure codimension $1$ in $X$.
666	  \item \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend\_iff\_order\_nonneg}
667	    --- \cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}, the Weil-divisor reformulation. Re-uses

670	\end{enumerate}
671	
672	\paragraph{Mathlib readiness audit.} At commit \texttt{b80f227} the prover should
673	verify:
674	\begin{itemize}
675	  \item \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.domain} is shipped at
676	    \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap}; its complement
677	    \texttt{indeterminacyLocus} is a one-line definition.
678	  \item The valuative criterion of properness in Mathlib form is
679	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper.valuative\_criterion} (or analogous); the
680	    prover invokes this in Step~1 of \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}'s proof.
681	  \item Mathlib's smoothness predicate
682	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth} bundles the regular-local-rings hypothesis; the
683	    DVR-at-codim-1 lemma reduces to ``regular local of dim 1 = DVR''
684	    (\texttt{IsRegularLocalRing.isDiscreteValuationRing\_of\_dim\_one} or similar).
685	  \item Mathlib's product-of-smooth-is-smooth instance,
686	    \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.comp\_pullback} / equivalent, is needed in
687	    \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy}'s Sub-step~4 to apply the Weil-divisor
688	    machinery to $X \times X$.
689	  \item The local-cohomology vanishing in Step~2 of
690	    \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}'s proof
691	    ($H^0_{\{x\}}(V, \mathcal O_X) = H^1_{\{x\}}(V, \mathcal O_X) = 0$ at a
692	    depth-$\geq 2$ point) is a substantial Mathlib gap as of \texttt{b80f227}; the
693	    prover's options are (i) prove the depth-$\geq 2$ extension property as a local
694	    lemma using the \texttt{Module.depth} / Auslander--Buchsbaum machinery of
695	    \cref{chap:Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum}, or (ii) reformulate
696	    \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} in the weaker ``surface'' form
697	    (where $X$ has $\dim 2$, so codim-$\geq 2$ means closed points only and the
698	    extension is the classical Hartshorne~III.7.1 ``smooth-surface point removal''
699	    argument). For the consumer \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} only
700	    the surface case is needed in practice (the symmetric product $C^{(g)}$ is
701	    smooth, and the Abel--Jacobi map's intermediate-step indeterminacy lives on a
702	    smooth surface inside $C^{(g)} \times C^{(g)}$).
703	\end{itemize}
704	
705	No new core axiom is required. The proof of \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} is
706	purely Weil-divisor manipulation on a smooth variety and is the cleanest part of
707	the chapter; the substance of the Lean engineering is in
708	\cref{thm:codim_one_extension}'s proof, both halves of which rest on Mathlib API
709	gaps the prover must either fill in or work around.
710	
711	\section{Out of scope}
712	\label{sec:codim1_out_of_scope}
713	
714	This chapter packages \emph{only} the two extension inputs and the Weil-divisor
715	reformulation needed for A.4.a. The following adjacent content is explicitly out of
716	scope:
717	\begin{itemize}
718	  \item \textbf{The Auslander--Buchsbaum formula and the regular $\Rightarrow$
719	    Cohen--Macaulay corollary.} Proved in \cref{chap:Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum}
720	    as \cref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum} and \cref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}; this
721	    chapter only consumes them (in Step~2 of \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}; see
722	    \cref{rmk:codim1_role_of_ab}).
723	  \item \textbf{Milne Theorem~3.2 itself.} The combination of
724	    \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} and \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} into the
725	    statement ``a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety
726	    extends'' lives in \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} as
727	    \cref{thm:rational_map_to_av_extends}; this chapter only supplies the two inputs.
728	  \item \textbf{Milne Theorem~3.4 / Corollary~3.7 / Theorem~3.8 / Proposition~3.9 /
729	    Proposition~3.10.} The remainder of Milne~\S I.3 (the ``map between AVs is
730	    translated homomorphism'' family of results, and the
731	    $\mathbb A^1 \dashrightarrow A$ / unirational$\dashrightarrow A$ constancy
732	    statements) is downstream of \cref{thm:rational_map_to_av_extends} but is not
733	    needed by A.4 in the project's current routing: the genus-$0$ base case is
734	    handled via the proven Rigidity Lemma + $\mathbb G_m$-scaling shortcut
735	    (\cref{chap:RigidityKbar}), and the positive-genus arm consumes only the bare
736	    Theorem~3.2 statement.
737	  \item \textbf{Weil-divisor degree on $X \times X$ for $X$ a curve.} The proof of
738	    \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} invokes the Weil-divisor apparatus on the
739	    smooth surface $X \times X$ but does \emph{not} need the degree map of
740	    \cref{def:divisor_degree} (which is curve-specific); only the codim-$1$
741	    decomposition $\div(f) = \div(f)_0 - \div(f)_\infty$ on a nonsingular surface is
742	    used. The bilinear degree formula on $X \times X$ for $X$ a curve (intersection
743	    numbers, Hartshorne~V.1) is out of scope.
744	  \item \textbf{Cartier divisors and the Cartier/Weil comparison.} On a smooth variety
745	    they coincide and the chapter uses Weil throughout; the Cartier formalism is not
746	    introduced.
747	  \item \textbf{Generalisation to non-algebraically-closed base fields.} The
748	    project's positive-genus arm runs over $\bar k$ throughout; descent of the
749	    extension to a non-algebraically-closed base, if ever needed, would require
750	    Galois-descent machinery and is not in scope.
751	  \item \textbf{Resolution of indeterminacy by blow-ups} (Hironaka-style, for higher
752	    indeterminacy codimensions on a smooth variety with a non-group-variety target):
753	    out of scope. The combination of \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} and
754	    \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} for $G$ a group variety is sharper than any
755	    blow-up-based approach because it produces an extension on the original $X$
756	    rather than on a blow-up; no blow-up apparatus is needed.
757	\end{itemize}

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.SpanRankOperations
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.DiscreteValuationRing.Basic
10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.KrullsHeightTheorem
11	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.KrullDimension.Field
12	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.KrullDimension.PID
13	
14	/-!
15	# Regular local rings
16	
17	For a Noetherian local ring `R`, we define `IsRegularLocalRing` as
18	`(maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R`. This definition is equivalent to
19	the dimension of the cotangent space over the residue field being equal to `ringKrullDim R`,
20	(see `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`).
21	
22	# Main Definition and Results
23	
24	* `IsRegularLocalRing` : A Noetherian local ring is regular if
25	  `(maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R`,
26	  i.e. its maximal ideal can be generated by `dim R` elements.
27	
28	* `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace` : the equivalence of `IsRegularLocalRing` and
29	  `Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) = ringKrullDim R`
30	
31	-/
32	
33	@[expose] public section
34	
35	open IsLocalRing
36	
37	/-- A Noetherian local ring is said to be regular if its maximal ideal
38	can be generated by `dim R` elements. -/
39	@[stacks 00KU]
40	class IsRegularLocalRing (R : Type*) [CommRing R] : Prop extends
41	    IsLocalRing R, IsNoetherianRing R where
42	  spanFinrank_maximalIdeal : (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R
43	
44	variable (R : Type*) [CommRing R]
45	
46	lemma isRegularLocalRing_iff [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R] :
47	    IsRegularLocalRing R ↔ (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R :=
48	  ⟨fun ⟨h⟩ ↦ h, fun h ↦ ⟨h⟩⟩
49	
50	namespace IsRegularLocalRing
51	
52	lemma of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
53	    (le : (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank ≤ ringKrullDim R) : IsRegularLocalRing R :=
54	  (isRegularLocalRing_iff _).mpr (le_antisymm le (ringKrullDim_le_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal R))
55	
56	variable {R} in
57	lemma of_ringEquiv [IsRegularLocalRing R] {R' : Type*} [CommRing R']
58	    (e : R ≃+* R') : IsRegularLocalRing R' := by
59	  have := e.isLocalRing
60	  have := isNoetherianRing_of_ringEquiv R e
61	  rwa [isRegularLocalRing_iff, ← ringKrullDim_eq_of_ringEquiv e, ← map_ringEquiv_maximalIdeal e,
62	    Ideal.spanFinrank_map_eq_of_ringEquiv, ← isRegularLocalRing_iff]
63	
64	lemma iff_finrank_cotangentSpace [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R] :
65	    IsRegularLocalRing R ↔ Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) = ringKrullDim R := by
66	  rw [isRegularLocalRing_iff, spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace]
67	
68	instance [IsLocalRing R] [IsDomain R] [IsPrincipalIdealRing R] : IsRegularLocalRing R := by
69	  by_cases isf : IsField R
70	  · let _ := isf.toField
71	    simp [isRegularLocalRing_iff, maximalIdeal_eq_bot]
72	  · apply of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le
73	    rcases IsPrincipalIdealRing.principal (maximalIdeal R) with ⟨x, hx⟩
74	    simpa only [(IsPrincipalIdealRing.ringKrullDim_eq_one R) isf,
75	      Nat.cast_le_one, ← Set.ncard_singleton x, hx] using
76	        Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite (Set.finite_singleton x)
77	
78	end IsRegularLocalRing

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus
8	
9	/-!
10	# Weil divisors on a smooth proper curve (RR.1)
11	
12	This file is the **RR.1** sub-build chapter for the project's headline
13	`genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geom-irred genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is
14	isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma in `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`).
15	
16	Mathlib `b80f227` ships no `WeilDivisor` on a scheme; adjacent pieces
17	(`MeromorphicOn.divisor`, `CommRing.Pic`, `Scheme.RationalMap`) cover different
18	ground. This file is therefore **project-bespoke**, scaffolding the formal-sum data
19	type `Div(X) = ⨁_{Y ⊂ X codim 1} ℤ` on a Noetherian integral scheme `X` satisfying
20	Hartshorne's condition `(*)`, the principal-divisor homomorphism
21	`div : K(X)^× → Div(X)` on a curve, the degree map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ` on a smooth
22	proper curve over an algebraically closed field, the degree-zero of principal divisors
23	on a complete nonsingular curve (Hartshorne Cor. II.6.10), and the linear-equivalence
24	relation `D ~ D'`.
25	
26	## Status (iter-172 file-skeleton)
27	
28	This file is the **iter-172 Lane C** file-skeleton: each declaration carries the
29	intended signature (matching the blueprint `\lean{...}` pin) with a `sorry` body.
30	The bodies are iter-173+ work after the sibling chapters `RR.2`
31	(`RiemannRoch_RRFormula.tex`), `RR.3` (`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`), and `RR.4`
32	(`RiemannRoch_RationalIsoP1.tex`) land.
33	
34	The 9 pinned declarations are:
35	
36	1. `Scheme.WeilDivisor` — free abelian group on prime divisors (Definition).
37	2. `Scheme.RationalMap.order` — order of a rational function along a prime divisor.
38	3. `Scheme.WeilDivisor.ofClosedPoint` — Weil divisor associated to a closed point.
39	4. `Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree` — degree map over an algebraically closed base.
40	5. `Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree_hom` — degree is a group homomorphism (Theorem).
41	6. `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal` — principal divisor of a rational function.
42	7. `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_hom` — `div` is a group homomorphism (Theorem).
43	8. `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero` — `deg ∘ div = 0` on a complete curve.
44	9. `Scheme.WeilDivisor.LinearEquivalence` — linear equivalence of Weil divisors.
45	
46	## References
47	
48	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` (445 LOC, 9 pins).
49	Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, II §6 (pp. 130–137 + IV.1 pp. 294–296).
50	Stacks Project, tags 02RW (divisors), 02ME (order at a point), 0BE0 (degree),
51	0BE3 (principal divisors have degree zero on a complete nonsingular curve).
52	-/

58	open CategoryTheory Limits
59	
60	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
61	
62	/-! ## §1. Codim-1 cycle group / Weil divisor group
63	
64	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` (II §6, p. 130) is: `X` is a Noetherian integral
65	separated scheme that is regular in codimension one. Under `(*)`, prime divisors
66	are closed integral subschemes of codimension one, and the local ring at the
67	generic point of a prime divisor is a DVR with quotient field the function field
68	`K(X)`.
69	
70	A prime divisor is encoded by its **generic point** together with the
71	codimension-one witness `Order.coheight point = 1`: the data field `point : X`
72	selects the generic point of the closed integral subscheme, and the predicate
73	field `coheight` enforces codimension one in the specialisation preorder
74	(Hartshorne II §6 p. 130; blueprint pin `def:prime_divisor`). Integrality of the
75	closure is automatic, so no separate integrality witness is needed.
76	-/
77	
78	/-- A **prime divisor** on a scheme `X`: a closed integral subscheme of codimension
79	one, encoded (for a scheme satisfying Hartshorne's condition `(*)`) by its generic
80	point. On a curve, prime divisors correspond bijectively to closed points.
81	
82	The codimension-one witness is the predicate `Order.coheight point = 1` on the
83	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier` (Mathlib's convention is
84	`x ≤ y ↔ y ⤳ x`, so the generic point is the unique maximal element and a
85	generic point of a codim-1 closed integral subscheme has a length-one chain
86	above it). The closure `{point}̄` is automatically irreducible (so the reduced
87	induced subscheme structure is automatically integral); hence no separate
88	integrality field is needed.
89	
90	Blueprint reference: `def:prime_divisor` / `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6
91	p. 130; Stacks 02RW). -/
92	structure Scheme.PrimeDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) where
93	  /-- The generic point of the closed integral subscheme. -/
94	  point : X
95	  /-- Codimension-1 witness: `point` has coheight `1` in the specialisation
96	  preorder on `X.carrier`. Per iter-173 `wd-spec-refine` (`def:prime_divisor`). -/
97	  coheight : Order.coheight point = 1
98	
99	/-- The **Weil divisor group** of a scheme `X` satisfying Hartshorne's condition
100	`(*)`: the free abelian group on the set of prime divisors of `X`,

160	/-! ### Regular-in-codimension-one bridge class
161	
162	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` includes "regular in codimension one", i.e.\ every
163	prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a DVR stalk `O_{X,Y.point}`. Mathlib has no direct
164	typeclass for this; we package it as the project-bespoke class
165	`Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne`, with an instance synthesising the per-`Y`
166	Krull-dim-≤-1 condition required by `Scheme.RationalMap.order`. Blueprint pin
167	("Iter-173+ may introduce a `Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne` predicate to
168	abbreviate this"; chapter `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §2 "Standing hypothesis
169	`(*)` in the Lean encoding"). -/
170	
171	/-- Project-bespoke class encoding Hartshorne's "regular in codimension one"
172	clause of `(*)`: every prime divisor `Y` of `X` has a Krull-dim-≤-1 stalk. -/
173	class Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne (X : Scheme.{u}) : Prop where
174	  /-- The defining content: every prime divisor's stalk has Krull dimension at
175	  most one. (For a regular-in-codim-1 scheme the stalk is in fact a DVR; we
176	  only export the weaker Krull-dim bound here, sufficient to invoke
177	  `Ring.ordFrac`.) -/
178	  out : ∀ Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X, Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)
179	
180	/-- Bridge instance: from `[Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]`, typeclass
181	synthesis can derive `Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)` for every
182	prime divisor `Y`. -/
183	instance {X : Scheme.{u}} [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
184	    (Y : Scheme.PrimeDivisor X) :
185	    Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point) :=
186	  Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.out Y
187	
188	/-- **Hartshorne II.6.1**: for a nonzero rational function `f` on a Noetherian
189	integral scheme `X` satisfying `(*)`, the order function `Y ↦ ord_Y(f)` is
190	nonzero at only finitely many prime divisors `Y`. This is the well-definedness
191	side condition for `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal`.
192	
193	iter-177 status: this packages Hartshorne's Lemma 6.1, which Mathlib does not
194	ship. The body is a Mathlib-upstream-pending gap (Stacks tag `02RV` — for a
195	nonzero element `f ∈ K(X)^×` of a Noetherian integral scheme, only finitely
196	many height-one primes can divide either numerator or denominator); the proof
197	factors through `IsLocallyNoetherian X` + the principal-ideal generation of
198	height-1 primes + the finite irreducible-component decomposition of
199	`V(f₀) ∪ V(f∞)`. The chapter pins this as a separate sub-build deferral
200	(`RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` §5).
201	
202	The statement is generic in `f` (no `f ≠ 0` hypothesis is threaded): on
203	`f = 0` the function `Y ↦ ord_Y(0) = WithZero.log 0 = 0` has empty support,
204	which is finite, so the conclusion holds vacuously. -/
205	private theorem rationalMap_order_finite_support {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
206	    [IsLocallyNoetherian X] [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X]
207	    (f : X.functionField) :
208	    (Function.support (fun Y : X.PrimeDivisor =>
209	      Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f)).Finite := by
210	  sorry

219	divisor (it is closed, integral, of codimension one in the one-dimensional integral

theorem localRing_dvr_of_codim_one
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
    IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk.
  -- `Spec k̄` is locally Noetherian since `k̄` is a field (hence Noetherian).
  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts this to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`,
  -- from which the stalk inherits `IsNoetherianRing`.
  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
  -- The stalk of an integral scheme is a domain (Mathlib instance);
  -- the stalk of a locally ringed space is local (Mathlib instance);
  -- the stalk of a locally Noetherian scheme is Noetherian (Mathlib instance).
  -- These are all picked up via typeclass search.
  -- Reduce DVR to the `(maximalIdeal R).IsPrincipal` clause of the TFAE.
  -- Smoothness over a field would yield `IsRegularLocalRing` on the stalk,
  -- and a Noetherian regular local ring of Krull dimension `1` has a
  -- principal maximal ideal (`spanFinrank = ringKrullDim = 1`); combined
  -- with the integral-domain hypothesis and `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥` (from
  -- `Order.coheight z = 1`, hence the stalk is not a field), the DVR
  -- conclusion follows from `IsDiscreteValuationRing.mk` applied to the
  -- principal-ideal-ring structure.
  --
  -- The full chain `Smooth ⟹ IsRegularLocalRing` + `coheight = 1 ⟹
  -- ringKrullDim = 1` is a substantive Mathlib gap (Stacks tags 00PD /
  -- 02IZ); we package its conclusion locally as `principal_maximal`.
  have principal_maximal :
      Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) :=
    smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal X z _hz
  have ne_bot :
      IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ :=
    smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_ne_bot X z _hz
  -- Convert `IsPrincipal maxIdeal` + Noetherian local domain ⟹ `IsPrincipalIdealRing`
  -- via the TFAE: a Noetherian local ring with principal maximal ideal is a
  -- PIR (in fact a DVR once it is a domain and the maximal ideal is nonzero).
  haveI : IsPrincipalIdealRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
    refine ⟨fun I => ?_⟩
    by_cases hI : I = ⊥
    · exact hI ▸ ⟨0, by simp⟩
    -- Use TFAE entry: every nonzero ideal is a power of the maximal ideal
    -- ⟹ every ideal is principal (since the maximal ideal is principal).
    have hfield : ¬ IsField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
      intro hF
      apply ne_bot
      exact (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_eq_bot (R := X.left.presheaf.stalk z)).mpr hF
    have tfae := IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) hfield
    -- Entry 4: principal maximal ideal ⇔ DVR (entry 0).
    have h04 : IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ↔
        Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) :=
      tfae.out 0 4
    have hdvr : IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) :=
      h04.mpr principal_maximal
    -- From DVR we get principal ideal ring structure on every ideal.
    have := (IsDiscreteValuationRing.iff_pid_with_one_nonzero_prime
      (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)).mp hdvr
    exact this.1.principal I
  -- Now assemble the DVR conclusion.
  exact IsDiscreteValuationRing.mk ne_bot

/-- **Helper for `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`.** On a smooth integral variety
over an algebraically closed field, the stalk at a codimension-`1` point has a
*principal* maximal ideal. This packages the Mathlib gap "smooth ⟹
`IsRegularLocalRing` + regular local of Krull dimension `1` ⟹ principal
maximal ideal" (Stacks `00PD` / `02IZ`). The substantive content is the smoothness ⟹
regularity step, which the project's Mathlib version (commit `b80f227`) does not
yet ship. -/
private theorem smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
    Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) :=
  sorry

/-- **Helper for `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`.** On a smooth integral variety
over an algebraically closed field, the maximal ideal of the stalk at a
codimension-`1` point is nonzero (the stalk is not a field). Follows from
`Order.coheight z = 1`: a codim-`1` point sits strictly above some generic
point in the specialisation preorder, so the stalk's Krull dimension is `≥ 1`
and the stalk is not a field. -/
private theorem smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_ne_bot
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
    IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ :=
  sorry

input.new_string:
/-- **Helper for `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`.** On a smooth integral variety
over an algebraically closed field, the stalk at a codimension-`1` point has a
*principal nonzero* maximal ideal. Encodes the substantive geometric content
of "regular in codimension one": smoothness ⟹ `IsRegularLocalRing` at the
stalk (Stacks `00PD`), and a Noetherian regular local ring with
`ringKrullDim = 1` has `spanFinrank (maximalIdeal) = 1`, i.e.\ a principal
nonzero maximal ideal (Stacks `02IZ`). The full chain
`Smooth ⟹ IsRegularLocalRing` + `coheight z = 1 ⟹ ringKrullDim = 1` is a
substantive Mathlib gap (commit `b80f227` only ships the definition
`IsRegularLocalRing`, not the smooth-scheme bridge); we package the conclusion
locally as a single helper sorry. -/
private theorem smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
    Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) ∧
      IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ :=
  sorry

theorem localRing_dvr_of_codim_one
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
    IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk:
  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts the (automatic) `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)`
  -- to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk inherits
  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance). The stalk of an integral scheme is
  -- a domain (Mathlib instance), and stalks of schemes are always local (Mathlib
  -- instance); these are picked up automatically by typeclass search.
  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
  -- Extract the principal+nonzero-maximal-ideal helper (the Mathlib-gap content).
  obtain ⟨hprin, hne⟩ :=
    smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot X z _hz
  -- The stalk is a local Noetherian domain with principal maximal ideal:
  -- promote `IsPrincipal (maximalIdeal)` to a `IsPrincipalIdealRing` instance
  -- and conclude DVR via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.mk`.
  -- The TFAE entry "principal maximal ideal ⇔ DVR" handles the conversion
  -- once we know `¬ IsField` (which follows from `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥`).
  have hfield : ¬ IsField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
    intro hF
    exact hne ((IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_eq_bot (R := X.left.presheaf.stalk z)).mpr hF)
  have tfae := IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) hfield
  -- TFAE entry index 4 is `Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)`.
  exact ((tfae.out 0 4).mpr hprin)

/-- **Smooth + codim-1 ⇒ DVR.** For a nonsingular integral variety `X` over
an algebraically closed field `k̄` and a point `η : X.left` with
`Order.coheight η = 1`, the stalk `X.left.presheaf.stalk η` is a discrete
valuation ring (with fraction field the function field `K(X)`).

The proof reduces to "regular local ring of Krull dimension `1` is a DVR"
(Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing.isDiscreteValuationRing_iff` or analogous)
plus the smooth-variety fact that every local ring at a codim-1 point is
regular of dimension `1`.

Blueprint reference: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130;
Stacks `00PD` for the regular-of-dim-1 ⇔ DVR equivalence).

The proof factors through one private helper
`smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot` that packages the
substantive Mathlib gap (smoothness ⟹ regular local stalk + Krull-dim-1
bridge ⟹ principal nonzero maximal ideal); the rest assembles the DVR
instance via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE`. -/
section

/-- **Helper for `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`.** On a smooth integral variety
over an algebraically closed field, the stalk at a codimension-`1` point has a
*principal nonzero* maximal ideal. Encodes the substantive geometric content
of "regular in codimension one": smoothness ⟹ `IsRegularLocalRing` at the
stalk (Stacks `00PD`), and a Noetherian regular local ring with
`ringKrullDim = 1` has `spanFinrank (maximalIdeal) = 1`, i.e.\ a principal
nonzero maximal ideal (Stacks `02IZ`). The full chain
`Smooth ⟹ IsRegularLocalRing` + `coheight z = 1 ⟹ ringKrullDim = 1` is a
substantive Mathlib gap (commit `b80f227` only ships the definition
`IsRegularLocalRing`, not the smooth-scheme bridge); we package the conclusion
locally as a single helper sorry. -/
private theorem smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
    Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) ∧
      IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ :=
  sorry

theorem localRing_dvr_of_codim_one
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}

    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
    IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by

# Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean — iter-178 Lane 4 (CodimOne-BODIES)

## Summary

- **Sorry delta**: 4 → 3 file sorries (net -1; targeted -2 with 1 new private helper sorry).
- **Build**: green; file diagnostics = 3 warnings (no errors).
- **Axiom hygiene**: `extend_iff_order_nonneg` axiom-clean (kernel only);
  `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` carries `sorryAx` via 1 named helper.
- **Helper budget used**: 1 / 1 (per directive).

## Targets

### `Scheme.RationalMap.extend_iff_order_nonneg` (L346 → now compiles, no sorry)

**Status**: RESOLVED, axiom-clean.

#### Attempt 1
- **Approach**: Mathlib `mem_domain` is `x ∈ f.domain ↔ ∃ g, x ∈ g.domain ∧ g.toRationalMap = f`; project signature reorders the conjuncts (`g.toRationalMap = f ∧ x ∈ g.domain`). Direct `rw [mem_domain]` + swap-pair `Iff.intro`.
- **Result**: RESOLVED
- **Key lemma**: `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain` (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.RationalMap`).
- **Proof** (~3 LOC):
  ```lean
  rw [Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain]
  exact ⟨fun ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩ => ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩, fun ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩ => ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩⟩
  ```

### `Scheme.localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` (L195 → now `by` block + 1 helper sorry)

**Status**: PARTIAL — main proof body is complete; sole remaining work is
a single private helper that captures the substantive Mathlib gap.

#### Attempt 1
- **Approach**: Use `IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE` to reduce DVR to
  "Submodule.IsPrincipal (maximalIdeal R)". Set up the typeclass scaffolding:
  - `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left` via `LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom` (and the auto-instance `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)` since `k̄` is a Noetherian field).
  - `IsNoetherianRing` of the stalk via `instIsNoetherianRingCarrierStalkCommRingCatPresheafOfIsLocallyNoetherian`.
  - `IsLocalRing` of the stalk via `LocallyRingedSpace.instIsLocalRingCarrierStalkCommRingCatPresheaf`.
  - `IsDomain` of the stalk via `instIsDomainCarrierStalkCommRingCatPresheafOfIsIntegral`.
  - `¬ IsField R` via `IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq` applied to the helper's `≠ ⊥`.
  - `tfae.out 0 4` gives the equivalence `IsDiscreteValuationRing R ↔ Submodule.IsPrincipal (maximalIdeal R)`.
- **Result**: PARTIAL — main proof body lands; only the geometric content
  (smooth ⟹ regular local stalk + coheight 1 ⟹ Krull dim 1 ⟹ principal nonzero max ideal) is factored out as a single private helper sorry.
- **Mathlib gap quantified**: Mathlib `b80f227` ships `IsRegularLocalRing`
  (`Mathlib.RingTheory.RegularLocalRing.Defs`) and the cotangent-space
  characterisation `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`, but no
  bridge `Smooth ⟹ IsRegularLocalRing` on stalks of smooth schemes. Closing
  the helper requires either:
  - (a) building the bridge `Algebra.FormallySmooth k̄ R ⟹ IsRegularLocalRing R`
    for `R` a stalk over `k̄` (analogous to Stacks `00TT` / `00TX`), or
  - (b) prove the regularity from `Algebra.IsSmoothAt` + Jacobian criterion
    (Mathlib has `Algebra.IsSmoothAt` in `RingTheory.Smooth.Locus` but
    no bridge to `IsRegularLocalRing`).
  - The `coheight z = 1 ⟹ ringKrullDim (stalk z) = 1` step is also a
    Mathlib gap (Stacks `02IZ`).

### `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (L243) and `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (L284)

**Status**: out-of-scope per iter-178 Lane 4 directive (gated on
Auslander-Buchsbaum bodies + Milne's difference-map argument). Bodies
untouched.

## Helper introduced

```lean
private theorem smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
    Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) ∧
      IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ :=
  sorry
```

Captures the entire Mathlib gap (smoothness ⟹ regular local + dim 1 ⟹
principal nonzero max ideal) in a single named lemma. Once Mathlib ships
`Smooth ⟹ IsRegularLocalRing`, the helper body becomes ~10 LOC using
`IsLocalRing.finrank_CotangentSpace_eq_one_iff` and
`IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`.

## Lemmas found (relevant)

- `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.RationalMap.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain` (used).
- `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Noetherian.LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian` (used).
- `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.FunctionField.instIsDomainCarrierStalk*OfIsIntegral` (auto).
- `Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalRing.MaximalIdeal.Basic.IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq` (used).
- `Mathlib.RingTheory.DiscreteValuationRing.TFAE.IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE` (used; entry 0 ↔ 4).
- `Mathlib.RingTheory.DiscreteValuationRing.TFAE.IsLocalRing.finrank_CotangentSpace_eq_one_iff` (not used; alternative route).
- `Mathlib.RingTheory.RegularLocalRing.Defs.IsRegularLocalRing` (referenced; gap source).

## Negative search results

- `Smooth ⟹ IsRegularLocalRing` for schemes — NOT in Mathlib `b80f227`.
- `coheight ⟹ ringKrullDim of stalk` — NOT in Mathlib (Stacks `02IZ` unformalized).
- `IsRegularLocalRing` + `ringKrullDim = 1` ⟹ `IsDiscreteValuationRing` — NOT
  in Mathlib as a direct lemma, but recoverable in ~5 LOC via
  `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace` +
  `IsLocalRing.finrank_CotangentSpace_eq_one_iff`.

## Next-iter recommendation

The helper `smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot` becomes a
single concrete prover lane once **either** of these Mathlib upstreams lands:
- A `Smooth → IsRegularLocalRing` instance/lemma on scheme stalks.
- A `Algebra.IsSmoothAt R (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal A) → IsRegularLocalRing A`
  bridge (which is the algebraic content of Stacks `00TT`).

Until then, the helper is the cleanest factorization: every Mathlib upgrade
on the smoothness/regularity bridge will close it in one ~10 LOC body.
